title: Claim your AI Employee
notes: Seven plain-language questions, each written to capture more than one fact. The web form collects these plus the supervisor's name, agent name, and timezone, verifies the phone inline with Twilio Verify or a signed text-in link, then hands a manifest to the host provisioner.

consent.capture: checkbox on the form, over the text below; phone ownership is proven by Twilio Verify or a signed inbound-SMS claim link
consent.text: I agree to receive ongoing texts from AMTECH so my AI Employee can work with me. I understand it may connect to tools or accounts I approve, that I am responsible for how I use it, and that message and data rates may apply. Reply STOP to cancel or HELP for help.

verification.flow: form posts /send-code (Verify sends OTP), user enters code, form posts /verify-and-claim (Verify checks OTP). One verification, inline.
verification.note: No dedicated onboarding number needed for this; Verify uses managed telephony. A number is only needed for the optional sms-entry signpost.

web.flow: User opens /claim, fills the form, verifies the phone inline with a Twilio Verify code.

sms_inbound.flow: User texts a keyword (e.g. 'AI EMPLOYEE') to the AMTECH onboarding number. sms-entry validates the Twilio signature, mints a short-lived single-use HMAC-signed link, and replies with https://amtechai.com/claim?t=<token>. The /claim page validates the token via /claim/start-from-sms, locks the phone, and hides the code step. The inbound signature is the proof of ownership, so no second code is needed.
sms_inbound.note: Both doors converge on the same buildManifest -> persistClaim -> triggerProvision tail. The manifest records verification.method ('twilio_verify' or 'sms_inbound') and consent.channel ('web' or 'sms'). The onboarding number is reserved out of the per-client pool (claim_number.py reserved list / RESERVED_NUMBERS).

branch.question: Do you own a business? If yes, what kind?
branch.if_no: Personal-use path (out of scope for the business MVP; collect phone + intended use only).

questions.prompt: What is the business? Name it, say what you sell or build, and say how long it has been running.
Who is on the team? Give the headcount and the jobs people handle.
What repeat computer work wastes the most time right now?
What tools do you use for email, spreadsheets, estimates, invoices, scheduling, or customer notes?
What does the business roughly bring in, and what does a normal customer or job look like?
What customer or job do you want more of?
What customer, job, or request creates too much friction?

extra_fields.prompt: What do you want to call your AI employee?

field_mapping_note: The host maps the seven answer ids to flat manifest fields. By default this is a deterministic map (raw answer into the primary field, agent learns the rest from its brain). With provision_client.py --enrich, an LLM structures the answers into clean fields first. Either way the raw answers are stored verbatim in the brain.
